summary: Sharp, short two‑set show with smoking Scarlet > Fire > Let It Grow and solid soundboard recording (minor tape‑speed quirks).
review: The performance is a tight, high‑energy two‑set gig that shines on the Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain → Let It Grow sequence, lightning‑supplication jams and a monster Let It Grow. The soundboard source is generally sweet, though a few tape‑speed glitches appear in Around and Around and an audience version of Truckin' can be a little distracting.

ai_rating.rationale: 11 reviews give an average of ~4.3 stars, with most praising the music and noting only minor recording flaws; the weighted blend of strong show quality (70%) and good soundboard audio (30%) supports a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall sweet, clear mix; occasional tape‑speed problems in Around and Around (and to a lesser extent Johnny B. Goode) and an audience‑sourced Truckin' that is slightly distracting.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: A notably short two‑set set (one of the briefest ever), economical yet relentless. The first set builds nicely into a scorching Scarlet > Fire > Let It Grow, while the second set delivers wild jams (Truckin' → Wharf Rat) and a soaring space.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Consistently praised; his playing is described as solid and “abides” throughout the jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned as being under‑the‑weather on this night, but also lauded for a “planet‑level” vocal presence by some reviewers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work is highlighted for creating an immersive, watery feel during the fire segment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Hot drums and strong rhythmic drive are repeatedly noted, especially in the space sections.
